meta.description: Canonical Phenome Registry — stable functional targets above mechanisms, below condition profiles.

labels: Energy Stability Under Variable Fuel Conditions
reviewFlags.note: Near-duplicate energy/fuel stability labels — consider merging under one canonical phenome after manual review.
Stress Resilience (adaptive capacity) vs Stress Reactivity (response intensity) — related but not identical; do not merge automatically.
Motivation/Drive vs Behavioural Activation — related; remain separate unless manually merged.

phenomes.name: Focus / Attention Stability
phenomes.description: Capacity to sustain, shift, and regulate attention across changing cognitive demands.
phenomes.publicSummary: How steadily a person can hold, shift, and return attention when tasks or distractions change.

phenomes.name: Motivation / Drive
phenomes.description: Capacity to initiate effort, pursue goals, and maintain goal-directed behaviour.
phenomes.publicSummary: How readily a person starts effort, pursues goals, and stays engaged with what matters to them.

phenomes.name: Emotional Regulation
phenomes.description: Capacity to modulate emotional responses and maintain affective stability.
phenomes.publicSummary: How well emotional responses can be modulated so mood and reactivity stay within a workable range.

phenomes.name: Cognitive Energy Stability
phenomes.description: Capacity to maintain mental energy and avoid cognitive energy crashes across the day.
phenomes.publicSummary: How evenly mental energy holds up through the day without sharp crashes or fog.

phenomes.name: Recovery Capacity
phenomes.description: Capacity to restore function after cognitive, emotional, metabolic, or physical load.
phenomes.publicSummary: How effectively function returns after sustained cognitive, emotional, metabolic, or physical demand.

phenomes.name: Stress Resilience
phenomes.description: Capacity to buffer, adapt to, and recover from stress exposure.
phenomes.publicSummary: How well the system buffers stress, adapts under pressure, and returns toward baseline afterward.

phenomes.name: Metabolic Resilience
phenomes.description: Capacity to maintain function under changing metabolic, energetic, or physiological demand.
phenomes.publicSummary: How well the body and brain maintain stable function when energy demand, fuel availability, or metabolic conditions change.

phenomes.name: Sleep / Calming Tone
phenomes.description: Capacity to downshift arousal and support sleep-compatible physiological regulation.
phenomes.publicSummary: How readily arousal can downshift toward calmer, sleep-compatible physiological states.

phenomes.name: Reward Regulation
phenomes.description: Capacity to regulate reward sensitivity, reinforcement learning, and reward-seeking behaviour.
phenomes.publicSummary: How reward sensitivity, reinforcement learning, and reward-seeking behaviour are regulated over time.

phenomes.name: Behavioural Activation
phenomes.description: Capacity to translate motivation into action, task initiation, and sustained behavioural engagement.
phenomes.publicSummary: How effectively motivation converts into starting tasks and sustaining behavioural engagement.

phenomes.name: Cognitive Clarity
phenomes.description: Capacity to maintain clear, organised mental processing without excessive fog or confusion.
phenomes.publicSummary: How clear and organised thinking feels — distinct from attention stability or raw mental energy.

phenomes.name: Energy Stability Under Variable Conditions
phenomes.description: Capacity to maintain energy output when nutritional, physiological, or environmental conditions change.
phenomes.publicSummary: How steadily energy production holds when conditions around nutrition, physiology, or demand shift.

phenomes.name: Energy Stability Under Variable Fuel Conditions
phenomes.description: Capacity to maintain ATP-linked energy supply when available fuel substrates or macronutrient context change.
phenomes.publicSummary: How well energy supply holds when glucose, fat, ketone, or mixed fuel availability changes.

phenomes.name: Hormonal Volatility
phenomes.description: Stability of hormone-linked signalling patterns that influence mood, cognition, and physiological regulation.
phenomes.publicSummary: How stable hormone-linked signalling feels over time — as distinct from a single hormone mechanism.

phenomes.name: Stress Reactivity
phenomes.description: Intensity and pattern of physiological and affective responses to stressors.
phenomes.publicSummary: How strongly and quickly the system reacts to stress — distinct from longer-term stress resilience.
